title: Fixed-degree poly-Laplacian combinations
summary: Packages finite sums of rank-r Laplacian words with a common total degree, proves their common output weight under homogeneity interfaces, and derives tangentiality from an explicit defect expansion and coefficient cancellation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Fixed total degree
mathematicalContent.body: The data consist of input weights w, a finite set S of Laplacian words, coefficients c_W, and a proof that every supported word has total degree N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Common output weight
mathematicalContent.body: Because all supported words have degree N, they share the predicted output homogeneity Σᵢwᵢ−2N. The proof uses the ambient algebraic, product-homogeneity, and finite-sum homogeneity interfaces.

mathematicalContent.heading: Defect cancellation
mathematicalContent.body: An abstract expansion expresses P(F)−P(G) modulo Q as obstruction operators multiplied by support-sums of word-level defect coefficients. If every such coefficient sum vanishes, P is tangential.
mathematicalContent.formula: \sum_{W\in S}c_Wd_{W,i,k}=0\ \forall i,k\quad\Longrightarrow\quad F\sim_wG\Rightarrow P(F)\equiv P(G)\pmod Q.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a finite coefficient system of rank-r Laplacian words, all having total degree N, together with their input weights.

correspondence.leanType: def outputWeight {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : ℝ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the common predicted output weight of the combination.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forms the finite multi-operator represented by the supported words.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ W ∈ D.support, D.coeff W * W.toOperator CalConf F U
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the combination at an input tuple F and ambient point U.

correspondence.leanType: theorem expectedOutputWeight_eq_outputWeight_of_mem {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {W : LaplacianWord r} (hW : W ∈ D.support) : W.expectedOutputWeight CalConf D.inputWeights = D.outputWeight CalConf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that every supported word has the same expected output weight as the whole fixed-degree combination.
Packages the additional principle that a finite sum of operators with one output weight has that same output weight.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \bigl(P_i:\mathcal E[w]\to\mathcal E[w_{\mathrm{out}}]\ \forall i\in S\bigr)\Longrightarrow\sum_{i\in S}c_iP_i:\mathcal E[w]\to\mathcal E[w_{\mathrm{out}}].

correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) (Hsum : OperatorSumHomogeneityIdentities CalConf) : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.inputWeights (D.outputWeight CalConf)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under the algebraic, product, and finite-sum homogeneity interfaces, proves the weight mapping of the fixed-degree combination.

correspondence.leanType: structure PolyDefectExpansion {r : ℕ} {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (K : Finset κ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a word-level boundary-defect expansion in a finite obstruction basis.

correspondence.leanType: def SatisfiesDefectCancellation {r : ℕ} {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {K : Finset κ} (E : PolyDefectExpansion D CalConf K) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines cancellation by requiring every obstruction coefficient in the expansion to vanish.

correspondence.leanType: theorem isTangentialAtWeights_of_defect_cancellation {r : ℕ} {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) {K : Finset κ} (E : PolyDefectExpansion D CalConf K) (hcancel : SatisfiesDefectCancellation D CalConf E) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.inputWeights
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves tangentiality from the supplied defect expansion and its coefficient-cancellation equations.

correspondence.leanType: def toWeightedMultiTangentialOperator {r : ℕ} (D : PolyLaplacianCombinationData r) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.inputWeights (D.outputWeight CalConf)) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.inputWeights) : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages a combination whose weight mapping and tangentiality have been proved as a weighted multi-tangential operator.

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The central geometric input is abstract: ProductHomogeneityIdentities, OperatorSumHomogeneityIdentities, and PolyDefectExpansion are supplied rather than derived in this file. Tangentiality is therefore verified under those interfaces and explicit cancellation hypotheses.
